company: Prosus N.V. (forced seller of Delivery H
bull_thesis: The bull case is not really about food delivery - it is that a cheap holding company closes the gap to what it is worth. Prosus is a roughly 130-billion-euro holding company (a firm whose value is the sum of its stakes) trading at a wide discount to that net asset value (NAV), currently quoted around 30-35%. About 80% of NAV is a ~23% stake in Tencent, the Chinese technology giant, so owning Prosus is a cheaper, leveraged way to own Tencent. Management is actively narrowing the discount with an open-ended share buyback (no fixed end date) that has returned close to 42 billion US dollars since June 2022 and lifted NAV-per-share about 18% versus doing nothing, backed by more than 13 billion euros of gross cash. On the named catalyst itself, the news is genuinely favorable: the EU-mandated sale of the Delivery Hero (the German-listed food-delivery group) stake is being executed at PREMIUMS, not as a fire sale - 4.5% sold to Uber at 20 euros in April and ~5% to Aspex at 22 euros in May - and Uber has tabled an indicative ~33 euro bid for all of Delivery Hero, so the residual ~17% has become a strategically-sold, possibly bid-target asset rather than a distressed line. The EU also pushed the deadline to 11 October 2026 and Prosus is asking to keep the stake. If Tencent steadies and the discount narrows partway, the stock re-rates toward the ~46 euro board target (analyst average is higher still).
bear_thesis: The bear case is that the labeled catalyst does not actually move Prosus, and the thing that does move it is uncapped. First, the mismatch: the forced selling is of DELIVERY HERO shares, not Prosus shares, so there is no supply overhang ON Prosus to clear. And Delivery Hero is now a low-single-digit-percent sliver of Prosus NAV (already cut from ~26% to ~17%), so however it resolves - sold, kept, or waived - the per-share impact on Prosus is trivial. The event has been pinned onto the wrong ticker. Second, the real driver is Tencent and the China-technology tape: with ~80% of NAV in one Chinese stock, Prosus is an unhedged proxy on Chinese regulatory and macro risk, and that is exactly what took the shares from ~50 euros in January 2026 to ~37 now. The holding-company discount has persisted for years and can stay wide or widen; there is no hard date that forces it shut. So the downside is not bounded by any deal floor - it floats with Tencent's own price, which can keep falling. The Uber bid for Delivery Hero is also only indicative (no signed agreement, regulators ahead) and could collapse.
sop_bull: Event-win value ~46 euros, derived from NAV with the discount partly closed. NAV is ~80% the ~23% Tencent stake (liquid, marked daily), plus more than 13 billion euros of gross cash, plus listed/unlisted e-commerce, classifieds and fintech holdings, plus the Delivery Hero remnant - which is being monetized at premiums (20-22 euros realized) and so ADDS to NAV rather than subtracting. Live ~37-39 euros embeds a ~30-35% discount; removing the overhang plus the NAV-accretive buyback narrows it partway (well short of full NAV), supporting ~46 euros, roughly +18-25% from here.
sop_bear: There is no event-specific floor because there is no event payout to Prosus. The floor is fundamental: Prosus's own NAV, which is ~80% Tencent. At today's price the stock already sits at a ~30-35% discount to NAV, more than 13 billion euros of gross cash backs it, and the open-ended buyback puts a standing bid underneath - the safest floor profile in the funnel (net cash, no going-concern, no dilution, no covenant wall, no contingent-value-right games). But because that floor is a function of Tencent's price rather than a fixed deal value, it is NOT a defined-risk floor. The board's quoted floor of ~37.4 euros is essentially the current price, so the asymmetry to the floor is roughly zero, and a renewed China-tech drawdown takes both Tencent and the floor down together.
sop_breakdown: Probability-weighted base case. ~80% of NAV is the ~23% Tencent stake (liquid), plus >13bn euros gross cash, plus e-commerce/classifieds/fintech holdings, plus the Delivery Hero remnant (~17%, monetized at 20-22 euro premiums - immaterial either way). Target ~46 euros (overhang removed + partial discount-narrowing + buyback accretion) weighted against a floor ~37.4 euros (deep-discount net-cash holdco). A roughly 47/53 weight toward the floor (~0.47 x 46.2 + 0.53 x 37.4) lands near 41.5 euros; the skew to the floor reflects that the labeled catalyst is immaterial to Prosus and the dominant discount driver (Tencent / China) is untouched by it.
risk_reward: On the board's numbers (live ~39.3, target 46.21, floor 37.37) upside-to-target is ~+18% and downside-to-floor ~-5%, a headline ~3.6:1. But that floor is not a real defined-risk floor - it is essentially today's price and it moves with Tencent. On the current trade (~37) the stock already sits at or below the quoted floor, so the labeled event asymmetry has effectively collapsed. The reward to NAV (~46) is real (~+20%) but the downside is open-ended via China beta, not bounded by any deal. As an event trade the asymmetry is weak; as a value position the margin of safety to NAV is genuine but Tencent-dependent.
catalyst_summary: EU-mandated Delivery Hero divestment, deadline extended to 11 Oct 2026 - executed at premiums (Uber 20 / Aspex 22 euros) into an indicative Uber ~33 euro bid for Delivery Hero - but it is a Delivery Hero event, immaterial to Prosus NAV (~80% Tencent), already ~two-thirds done, and Prosus is asking the EU to keep the stake; it does not force a Prosus re-rate.
dated_milestone: EU divestiture deadline extended to 11 October 2026 (waiver granted ~2 Jun 2026, twice extended); Uber ~33 euro/share indicative bid for all of Delivery Hero tabled 23 May 2026 (non-binding, BaFin/antitrust pending); Prosus has petitioned the EU (late May 2026) to drop the remaining requirement.
forcing_function: There is no real forcing function for a PROSUS re-rate. The only hard date (11 Oct 2026 Delivery Hero deadline) forces selling of Delivery Hero, not Prosus, is immaterial to Prosus NAV, and is being deliberately defanged by Prosus's request to keep the stake. The genuine value lever - narrowing the NAV discount via the open-ended buyback - is a perpetual grind with no hard date, ultimately gated on Tencent.
consensus_delta: The market is not mispricing an event here - it correctly prices Prosus as a Tencent / China-tech proxy at a structural holding-company discount. The error is on our board's side: treating a 'forced seller of Delivery Hero' label as a Prosus catalyst. The forced selling lands on Delivery Hero shares, not Prosus; Delivery Hero is a low-single-digit-percent piece of Prosus NAV already mostly divested - and divested at premiums (Uber 20, Aspex 22 euros), with Prosus now asking the EU to keep the rest. Where one could genuinely disagree with the street is the longer-run value question: a ~30-35% discount to a Tencent-heavy NAV plus an 18%-NAV-accretive buyback and >13bn euros of cash is a real value setup. But that is a discount-convergence thesis, not an expectations-arbitrage on a dated event - and anyone betting on full discount closure is offside on the persistent China overhang.
valley_of_death: Near-term (3-9 months) the dominant risk is Tencent and the China-tech tape, not the Delivery Hero deadline. With ~80% of NAV in Tencent, any renewed Chinese regulatory action, macro weakness, or Hong Kong drawdown hits Prosus directly and uncapped - exactly the move that already took the stock from ~50 euros in January to ~37 now. There is no cash-burn hump or maturity wall (net cash >13bn euros, asset-rich, cash-generative), so the danger is purely mark-to-market on the Tencent stake, against which there is no hedge or deal floor. Secondary: Uber's indicative Delivery Hero bid could stall or collapse, but that is immaterial to Prosus. The diffuse, defanged catalyst means no single re-rate day - the spread can grind sideways while China sentiment dominates.
positioning_washout: No mechanical forced-selling in the PROSUS shareholder base - there is no dividend cut or index event forcing holders out. (The only forced selling in this story is Prosus itself selling Delivery Hero, which pressures Delivery Hero, not Prosus - and that supply is being absorbed at premiums by strategic buyers, Uber and Aspex, not dumped.) If anything the flow is supportive: the open-ended buyback is a standing, price-insensitive bid that mechanically shrinks the float and accretes NAV-per-share each quarter.
moderator_conclusion: This is a catalyst-mismatch, not a true special situation - which is why it lands at wait-and-watch (B) with low event conviction, not the aggressive entry reserved for a hard-dated binding event inside a tight window. Prosus is a sound, genuinely cheap holding company at a wide ~30-35% discount to a Tencent-dominated NAV, with a disciplined open-ended buyback and more than 13 billion euros of cash steadily narrowing that gap - a respectable long-term value position, and one with the best floor profile in the funnel (net cash, no going-concern, no dilution). But the event this funnel grades does not hold up for Prosus: the 'forced seller of Delivery Hero' catalyst is directionally aimed at Delivery Hero (not Prosus), Delivery Hero is immaterial to Prosus NAV, the divestment is already roughly two-thirds executed and at premiums, and Prosus is actively asking the EU to keep the remainder. The board's quoted floor (~37.4) is essentially today's price and is not a fixed deal floor - it floats with Tencent - so the defined-risk asymmetry the special-situation playbook needs is absent. As a dated event trade this is a pass on aggression; as a value holding it is a wait-and-accumulate on China-tech weakness or a clean sentiment washout. Net: do not size as an asymmetric event; treat as a Tencent-discount value play. The higher value rating than event conviction reflects the deep, mechanically-supported margin of safety to a liquid NAV - the floor carries this name even though the labeled event will not.
interrogator_dossier:
# Prosus N.V. (PRX.AS) - Forensic Dossier (Event-Driven Lens)

Special-situation review of the board's 'forced seller of Delivery Hero' supply-clearing catalyst: is it real, dated, binding, and load-bearing for the PROSUS price, and what is the verified downside floor.

## 1. The catalyst and the mismatch at its core
The Catalyst-Watch entry frames Prosus as a 'forced seller of Delivery Hero' on the supply-clears logic. The facts: when the EU cleared Prosus's ~4.1bn-euro takeover of Just Eat Takeaway (Aug 2025), it required Prosus to cut its Delivery Hero (DHER) stake below ~10%. So Prosus is a forced seller on a regulatory clock; the deadline has been extended twice and is now 11 Oct 2026 (granted ~2 Jun 2026 at majority-owner Naspers's request after a reported Uber bid for Delivery Hero). The mismatch: the shares being force-sold are DELIVERY HERO shares, not PROSUS shares. Forced-seller supply pressure lands on the thing being sold (Delivery Hero), not on Prosus. There is no forced selling of Prosus. The supply-clears trade, if anywhere, points at Delivery Hero - not Prosus.

## 2. Is the catalyst material to Prosus? No.
Prosus is a ~130bn-euro+ holding company whose NAV is dominated by a single asset: a ~23-24% Tencent stake, on the order of 80% of NAV. Delivery Hero is now a low-single-digit-percent sliver. Prosus has already sold it down in 2026 at PREMIUMS, not a fire sale: from ~26.3% to ~21.8% (4.5% to Uber at 20 euros, ~270m euros, April), then a further ~5% to activist Aspex at 22 euros (~335m euros, May), leaving ~17% - worth ~1.5-2bn euros against a 130bn-euro+ NAV, a rounding error. Uber has tabled an indicative ~33 euro bid for ALL of Delivery Hero (23 May 2026), so the residual is a strategically-sold, possibly bid-target asset. Whether Prosus sells the last slice, sells to Uber, or gets the remedy waived, the per-share impact on Prosus is trivial. The labeled catalyst cannot move Prosus; it can move Delivery Hero. The funnel pinned a Delivery-Hero event onto the Prosus ticker.

## 3. What actually drives Prosus
(1) Tencent - with ~80% of NAV in one Chinese tech stock, Prosus is a leveraged proxy on Tencent / the China-tech tape; the stock fell from ~50 euros in Jan 2026 to ~37 now on Tencent weakness and a widening holdco discount, not food delivery. (2) The holding-company discount - Prosus persistently trades ~30-35% below the sum of its parts. Closing it is the real value lever; the tool is an open-ended buyback running since June 2022 that returned ~42bn US dollars and lifted NAV-per-share ~18%, backed by >13bn euros gross cash. But a buyback is a perpetual grind, not a hard-dated event.

## 4. Catalyst status, credibility, red flags
Status: the Delivery Hero remedy is hard and dated (11 Oct 2026) but is aimed at Delivery Hero not Prosus, is immaterial to Prosus NAV, is largely already executed (and at premiums), and Prosus is asking the EU to keep the remainder (late May 2026). A catalyst the issuer is trying to cancel, that is ~two-thirds done, and that moves neither side of the Prosus tape, is spent for our purposes. Credibility: management is highly credible operationally (premiums extracted, deadline extension won, NAV-accretive buyback, >42bn US dollars returned, net-cash balance sheet) - relevance, not competence, is the problem. Downside floor: no event-specific floor because no event payout to Prosus; the only floor is fundamental NAV, ~80% Tencent, so it floats with Tencent rather than being a fixed deal value. The board's quoted floor (~37.4) is essentially today's price - asymmetry to floor ~zero. Red flags for the EVENT thesis specifically: mislabeled catalyst (Delivery Hero event on Prosus ticker); the cited spread (~39.3 vs 46.21 vs 37.37) is a generic NAV-discount-convergence number, not an event spread, and live price has drifted to ~37; the real risk (Tencent / China beta) is uncapped and unhedged; the Uber DHER bid is only indicative. None are fraud/going-concern flags - Prosus is net-cash and sound - they are reasons the EVENT thesis fails.

## 5. Verdict for the director
Prosus is a legitimately cheap, well-run holding company at a wide discount to a Tencent-dominated NAV, with a disciplined buyback and >13bn euros cash - real merit as a long-term value holding, with the safest floor profile in the funnel. But as the event-driven special situation this funnel grades, it does not qualify: the forced selling is of Delivery Hero (not Prosus), Delivery Hero is immaterial to Prosus NAV, the remedy is mostly executed at premiums and Prosus is trying to keep the rest, and there is no dated event that forces a Prosus re-rate or any fixed downside floor. The single thing to weigh: this is a Tencent-discount value play wearing a borrowed catalyst label, not a defined-risk arbitrage. Do not size as an asymmetric event trade.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 2 | TRAJECTORY: STABLE | MOAT: NONE | MOAT_TREND: STABLE | SECULAR_THREAT: manageable
